questions.text: Which of the following statements about derived units is correct?

options.A: Derived units are independent of fundamental units.
options.B: Derived units are formed by combining fundamental units.
options.C: Derived units are used only in theoretical physics.
options.D: Derived units have no relation with physical quantities.

questions.explanation: Derived units are obtained by combining fundamental units through multiplication or division. For example, velocity is measured in metre per second, formed from metre and second.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is not correct regarding the unit of force?

options.C: It is obtained from mass and acceleration

questions.explanation: Force is not a fundamental quantity; it is derived from mass and acceleration (F = ma). Its SI unit is newton, so calling it a fundamental unit is incorrect.

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs contains only scalar quantities?
questions.explanation: Speed and distance are scalars (magnitude only). Velocity, force, displacement, and acceleration are vectors (magnitude + direction).

questions.text: Which of the following situations correctly represents zero velocity but non‑zero speed?

options.A: A body moving with constant acceleration in a straight line
options.B: A body completing one full round on a circular track
options.C: A body at rest on the ground
options.D: A body moving in a straight line with constant speed

questions.explanation: After completing one full circle, displacement is zero, so velocity is zero, but distance travelled (speed) is non‑zero.

questions.text: Which of the following statements correctly explains the motion of a stone tied to a string and whirled in a circle?

options.A: The string provides an outward force that keeps the stone moving.
options.B: The string provides a force towards the centre that keeps the stone in circular motion.
options.C: The stone moves in a circle without any force acting on it.
options.D: The force acts only when the stone changes its speed.

questions.explanation: The string exerts a centripetal force towards the centre, continuously changing the direction of velocity, keeping the stone in circular motion.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct about centripetal force in uniform circular motion?

options.A: It changes both speed and direction of the body.
options.B: It changes only the speed of the body.
options.C: It changes only the direction of the body's velocity.
options.D: It has no effect on the motion of the body.

questions.explanation: In uniform circular motion, speed is constant; centripetal force only changes the direction of velocity.

questions.assertion: Passengers in a moving bus are thrown forward when the driver suddenly applies brakes.
questions.reason: The passengers' bodies tend to continue in their state of motion due to inertia.

options.A: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, but R is false
options.D: A is false, but R is true

questions.explanation: Newton's first law (inertia): when brakes are applied, the lower body stops with the bus, but the upper body tends to continue moving forward, throwing passengers forward. R correctly explains A.

questions.text: A person carries a stone from the Earth to the Moon. What happens to its mass and weight?

options.B: Mass remains the same, but weight decreases
options.C: Mass decreases, but weight remains the same
options.D: Both mass and weight remain the same

questions.explanation: Mass is invariant; weight depends on gravitational acceleration, which is less on the Moon, so weight decreases.

questions.text: During free fall near the surface of the Earth, the acceleration of a body is:

options.C: equal to acceleration due to gravity
options.D: different for heavy and light bodies

questions.explanation: All bodies fall with the same acceleration g (≈ 9.8 m/s²) in the absence of air resistance.

questions.text: Which of the following statements correctly explains why the soles of shoes and vehicle tyres are made rough?

options.A: Rough surfaces reduce all kinds of friction.
options.B: Rough surfaces increase friction and improve grip on the ground.
options.C: Rough surfaces make the body lighter during motion.
options.D: Rough surfaces are used only for decorative appearance.

questions.explanation: Rough surfaces increase friction, providing better grip and reducing slipping.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes fluid friction?

options.A: It acts only between solid surfaces
options.B: It acts when a body moves through a liquid or gas
options.C: It is independent of the speed of the body
options.D: It is always greater than static friction

questions.explanation: Fluid friction (drag) acts on objects moving through fluids; it depends on speed, shape, and fluid properties.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct about the Kelvin scale?

options.A: It has negative temperatures in common use
options.B: Its zero is based on the freezing point of water

questions.explanation: Kelvin scale starts at absolute zero (0 K), the lowest possible temperature. It does not use the degree symbol; unit is kelvin (K).

questions.text: Heat conduction is the process of transfer of heat:

options.A: by actual movement of particles from one place to another
options.B: from a hotter region to a colder region without bulk movement of matter

questions.explanation: Conduction transfers heat through direct contact without bulk movement; it is most effective in solids.

questions.text: Why does the handle of a metal spoon become hot when its other end is kept in hot tea?

options.A: Because hot tea rises upward through the spoon
options.B: Because heat travels through the metal by conduction
options.C: Because the spoon absorbs heat from the surroundings
options.D: Because heat travels through the metal by convection

questions.explanation: Metals are good conductors; heat flows through the spoon from the hot end to the handle via conduction.

questions.text: A person can walk more easily on dry sand if he uses wide‑soled shoes because:

options.B: wide soles decrease pressure on the sand
options.C: wide soles increase the force exerted on the ground

questions.explanation: Wider soles increase area, reducing pressure (P = F/A), so they sink less and walking is easier.

questions.text: In a liquid at rest, pressure at a point depends mainly on:

options.A: the colour of the liquid and the shape of the vessel
options.B: the depth of the point below the liquid surface
options.C: the width of the container only
options.D: the surface area of the liquid only

questions.explanation: Pressure in a static fluid increases with depth (P = ρgh), independent of container shape.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct?

options.A: Pitch depends on amplitude of sound
options.B: Pitch and loudness always increase together
options.C: Pitch depends on frequency, while loudness depends on amplitude
options.D: Pitch depends on the colour of the source

questions.explanation: Pitch is determined by frequency (higher frequency = higher pitch). Loudness is determined by amplitude.

questions.text: When an object is placed between the pole and the focus of a concave mirror, the image formed is:
questions.explanation: For a concave mirror, when object is between pole and focus, the image is virtual, erect, and enlarged (used as shaving mirror).

questions.text: Refraction of light occurs when light:

options.A: is reflected from a polished surface
options.B: passes from one medium to another medium
options.D: is absorbed completely by a black surface

questions.explanation: Refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one transparent medium to another, due to change in speed.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct about optical density and refractive index?

options.A: A medium with lower refractive index is optically denser
options.B: Optical density and refractive index are unrelated
options.C: A medium with higher refractive index is optically denser
options.D: Optical density depends only on colour of the medium

questions.explanation: Higher refractive index means light slows down more, so the medium is optically denser.

questions.assertion: Ohm's law is not valid if the temperature of the conductor changes significantly.
questions.reason: Resistance of a conductor may change with temperature.

options.A: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, but R is false
options.D: A is false, but R is true

questions.explanation: Ohm's law assumes constant physical conditions; resistance changes with temperature, so the law fails if temperature varies. R correctly explains A.

questions.text: A student notices that when too many electrical appliances are switched on at the same time, the fuse wire melts and the circuit breaks. What is the most appropriate explanation?

options.A: The fuse melts because voltage becomes zero in the circuit
options.B: The fuse melts because excessive current produces too much heat in it
options.C: The fuse melts because the appliances stop using electricity
options.D: The fuse melts because the neutral wire becomes shorter

questions.explanation: Excessive current heats the fuse wire beyond its melting point, breaking the circuit and protecting appliances.

questions.text: In a house, a short circuit suddenly occurs and sparks are seen near a damaged wire. Which statement best explains the situation?

options.A: A short circuit happens when current becomes too small to flow
options.B: A short circuit happens when live and neutral wires come into direct contact, causing a large current
options.C: A short circuit happens because the fuse stops working normally
options.D: A short circuit happens only when an appliance is switched off

questions.explanation: Direct contact between live and neutral wires creates a low‑resistance path, causing a very large current and sparks.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about magnetic poles is correct?

options.C: Like poles repel each other and unlike poles attract each other
options.D: Magnetic poles have no interaction with each other

questions.explanation: Like poles repel, unlike poles attract – a fundamental property of magnets.

questions.text: Which of the following devices works on the principle of magnetism?
questions.explanation: An electric motor uses the magnetic force on a current‑carrying coil to produce rotation.

questions.text: What happens to the velocity of a body while it is falling freely under gravity, neglecting air resistance?

options.A: It remains constant throughout the motion
options.D: It first increases and then becomes zero

questions.explanation: Under gravity, acceleration is constant (g), so velocity increases uniformly with time.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements correctly distinguishes mass from weight?

options.A: Mass changes from place to place, while weight remains constant
options.B: Mass is a force, while weight is the quantity of matter in a body
options.C: Mass is the quantity of matter in a body, while weight is the gravitational force acting on it
options.D: Mass and weight are both measured in newton

questions.explanation: Mass is the amount of matter (constant); weight is the gravitational force on that mass (varies with gravity).

questions.text: A swimmer is able to move forward in water because:

options.A: water pulls the swimmer forward without any reaction
options.B: the swimmer pushes water backward, and water pushes the swimmer forward
options.C: the swimmer moves forward because of the frictional force in water
options.D: the swimmer becomes lighter inside water, which makes him swim

questions.explanation: Newton's third law: action (push water backward) and reaction (water pushes swimmer forward).

questions.assertion: If the same force acts on two bodies of different masses, the lighter body will have greater acceleration.
questions.reason: According to Newton's second law, acceleration is inversely proportional to mass for a given force.

options.A: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, but R is false
options.D: A is false, but R is true

questions.explanation: F = ma => a = F/m. For same F, smaller mass gives larger acceleration. R correctly explains A.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about centripetal force is correct?

options.A: It acts along the direction of motion of the body.
options.B: It acts away from the centre of the circular path.
options.C: It acts towards the centre of the circular path.
options.D: It acts only when the speed of the body changes.

questions.explanation: Centripetal force always acts towards the centre of the circular path, changing only direction, not speed (in uniform circular motion).

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct about the unit of power?

options.A: It is a fundamental unit measured in joule
options.B: It is a derived unit measured in watt
options.C: It is equal to kilogram per metre
options.D: It does not depend on time

questions.explanation: Power = work/time; its SI unit is watt (J/s), a derived unit.

questions.text: Which of the following statements best explains why passengers in a bus tend to fall backward when the bus starts suddenly?

options.A: The passengers are pushed backward by the seat due to friction alone.
options.B: The upper part of the body tends to remain at rest because of inertia, while the lower part moves with the bus.
options.C: The bus moves faster than the force of gravity.
options.D: The passengers fall backward because their weight suddenly increases.

questions.explanation: Inertia: the lower body moves with the bus, but the upper body tends to stay at rest, causing a backward jerk.

questions.text: Which of the following statements correctly reflects the role of external force in Newton's first law?

options.A: External force is needed to maintain motion in all cases.
options.B: External force is needed only to start motion, not to stop it.
options.C: External force is needed to change the state of rest or uniform straight‑line motion of a body.
options.D: External force is needed only when the body is at rest.

questions.explanation: Newton's first law: a body continues in its state of rest or uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force to change that state.

questions.text: A body is said to be in free fall when:

options.A: it moves upward with constant speed
options.B: only gravitational force acts on it
options.C: it falls in air with no motion
options.D: no force acts on it at all

questions.explanation: Free fall means the only force acting is gravity; air resistance is neglected.

questions.text: Potential energy of a body is the energy possessed by it due to:
questions.explanation: Potential energy is stored energy due to position (gravitational) or configuration (elastic).

questions.text: Which of the following statements best explains why it is more difficult to walk on a sandy road than on a cemented road?

options.A: Sand always produces more friction than a hard road.
options.B: The foot gets less firm backward push from sand, so proper frictional support is reduced.
options.C: Sand reduces the weight of the body while walking.
options.D: Walking on sand becomes difficult because air resistance increases near the ground.

questions.explanation: Sand yields under the foot, giving less reaction force, reducing friction and making walking harder.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct regarding sliding friction?

options.A: It is always greater than static friction
options.B: It acts when a body slides over another surface
options.C: It acts only in liquids and gases
options.D: It is equal to rolling friction

questions.explanation: Sliding friction occurs when two surfaces slide over each other; it is generally less than static friction.

questions.text: The value of 50° C on the Fahrenheit scale is:
If the frequency of a vibrating source is increased, the sound produced becomes:
questions.explanation: Higher frequency increases pitch, making the sound more shrill.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct about the size of the image formed by a plane mirror?

options.A: It is always larger than the object
options.B: It is always smaller than the object
options.C: It is always equal to the size of the object
options.D: It depends on the colour of the object

questions.explanation: A plane mirror always forms an image of the same size as the object (magnification = 1).

questions.text: A pencil partly dipped in water appears bent at the water surface because of:
questions.explanation: The bending is due to refraction – light changes speed and direction when passing from water to air.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about refractive index is correct?

options.A: It tells how much heat a medium can absorb
options.B: It tells how much a medium slows down light compared to vacuum
options.C: It is equal to the wavelength of light in air
options.D: It remains zero for all transparent media

questions.explanation: Refractive index = speed of light in vacuum / speed in medium; higher value means light slows down more.

questions.assertion: If the current through a conductor becomes zero, the resistance of the conductor must also become zero.
questions.reason: According to Ohm's law, resistance is given by the ratio V/I.

options.A: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, but R is false
options.D: A is false, but R is true

questions.explanation: Zero current does not imply zero resistance; R can be finite with V=0 or open circuit. R (V/I) is true but does not explain A because A is false.

questions.text: A person touches the metal body of an electric iron that has developed a fault, but does not receive a shock because the appliance is properly earthed. What does earthing do in this case?

options.A: It increases the voltage of the appliance so that shock is avoided
options.B: It provides a low‑resistance path for leakage current to flow into the ground
options.C: It stops the appliance from using electric current
options.D: It converts alternating current into direct current

questions.explanation: Earthing provides a safe path for leakage current to ground, preventing electric shock.

questions.text: Which of the following materials is strongly attracted by a magnet?
questions.explanation: Iron is ferromagnetic and is strongly attracted to magnets.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about kinetic energy is incorrect?
statements: It is the energy possessed by a body due to its position.
It is independent of the speed of the body.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect (it describes potential energy). Statement 2 is incorrect (kinetic energy depends on speed). So both are incorrect.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct?
statements: A body in uniform motion covers unequal distances in equal intervals of time.
For a body in uniform motion, distance‑time graph is a straight line.
questions.explanation: Uniform motion means equal distances in equal times; statement 1 is false. Statement 2 is true (straight line with constant slope).

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct?
statements: The speed of a body in uniform motion increases continuously with time.
The distance‑time graph of uniform motion is always parallel to the time axis.
questions.explanation: Speed is constant, so statement 1 is false. A graph parallel to time axis means zero speed (rest), so statement 2 is also false. Thus none are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct regarding escape velocity from the Earth?
statements: It is independent of the mass of the projected body.
It has the same value regardless of the direction of projection, if air resistance is ignored.
questions.explanation: Escape velocity depends only on Earth's mass and radius, not on the projectile's mass or direction (in ideal case). Both statements are correct.

questions.text: Which of the following statements correctly describes the effect of doubling the speed of a body on its kinetic energy?

options.B: The kinetic energy becomes four times

questions.explanation: KE = ½mv²; doubling v makes v² four times larger, so KE becomes four times.

questions.text: When a magnesium ribbon is burnt in air, it produces a coloured flame.
questions.explanation: Magnesium burns with a dazzling white flame, forming MgO.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
I. Litmus solution is a colourless liquid, which is extracted from lichen.
II. A stain of curry on a white cloth becomes reddish‑brown when soap, which is acidic in nature, is scrubbed on it.
Identify the correct statement/s.
statements: Litmus solution is a colourless liquid, which is extracted from lichen.
A stain of curry on a white cloth becomes reddish‑brown when soap, which is acidic in nature, is scrubbed on it.
questions.explanation: Litmus is purple, not colourless. Soap is basic (not acidic), so curry stain turns reddish‑brown due to basicity. Both statements are incorrect.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements.
I. The ability of metals to be drawn into thin wires is called ductility.
II. The metals are good conductors of heat and thus have low melting points.
III. The metals that produce a sound on striking a hard surface are said to be sonorous.
Identify the correct statement/s.
statements: The ability of metals to be drawn into thin wires is called ductility.
The metals are good conductors of heat and thus have low melting points.
The metals that produce a sound on striking a hard surface are said to be sonorous.
questions.explanation: I is correct (ductility). III is correct (sonorous). II is incorrect because metals generally have high melting points, not low.

questions.text:
Fill in the blanks.
In _____, each carbon atom is bonded to three other carbon atoms in the same plane giving a hexagonal array. One of these bonds is a double‑bond, and thus the valency of carbon is satisfied.
questions.explanation: Graphite has a layered hexagonal structure where each carbon is bonded to three others (one double bond).

questions.text: Esters are most commonly formed by reaction of
questions.explanation: Esters are formed by the reaction of a carboxylic acid and an alcohol (esterification).

questions.question:
Consider the statements.
I. Saturated hydrocarbons will generally give a clean flame while burning.
II. Unsaturated carbon compounds will give a yellow flame with lots of black smoke.
Identify the correct statement/s.
statements: Saturated hydrocarbons will generally give a clean flame while burning.
Unsaturated carbon compounds will give a yellow flame with lots of black smoke.
questions.explanation: Saturated hydrocarbons burn with a clean blue flame (complete combustion). Unsaturated ones burn with a yellow sooty flame (incomplete combustion). Both are correct.

questions.text: A series of compounds in which the same functional group substitutes for hydrogen in a carbon chain is called
questions.explanation: A homologous series is a group of compounds with the same functional group, differing by a CH₂ unit.

questions.text: The process of forming a thick oxide layer of Aluminium, to make it corrosion‑resistant is known as
questions.explanation: Anodizing is an electrolytic process to thicken the natural oxide layer on aluminium, enhancing corrosion resistance.

questions.text: When Silver articles is exposed to air, it becomes black after some time. This is because a thin layer of ______ is formed.
questions.explanation: Silver reacts with H₂S in air to form black Ag₂S (silver sulphide).

questions.question:
Consider the following statements.
I. The sulphide ores are converted into oxides by heating strongly in the presence of excess air. This process is known as roasting.
II. The nitrate ores are changed into oxides by heating strongly in limited air. This process is known as calcination.
Identify the correct statement/s.
statements: The sulphide ores are converted into oxides by heating strongly in the presence of excess air. This process is known as roasting.
The nitrate ores are changed into oxides by heating strongly in limited air. This process is known as calcination.
questions.explanation: Roasting is for sulphide ores with excess air (I correct). Calcination is for carbonate ores, not nitrate ores; so II is incorrect.

questions.text: A visually challenged student has to perform a lab test to detect the presence of acid in a given solution. What will be the acid‑base indicator preferred by him?
questions.explanation: Clove oil is an olfactory indicator – its smell changes in acids/bases, useful for visually challenged students.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
Statement I: Hydrogen gas is not evolved when a metal reacts with nitric acid.
Statement II: HNO₃ is a strong oxidising agent.
statements: Hydrogen gas is not evolved when a metal reacts with nitric acid.
HNO₃ is a strong oxidising agent.

options.A: Both the statements are individually true and statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I.
options.B: Both the statements are individually true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I.
options.C: Statement I is true, but Statement II is false.
options.D: Statement I is false, but Statement II is true.

questions.explanation: Nitric acid is a strong oxidising agent; it oxidises H₂ to water, so no H₂ gas is evolved. II correctly explains I.

questions.text:
Consider the following chemical reaction: Al₂O₃ + X HCl → Y AlCl₃ + Z H₂O
Which of the following will be the values of the coefficients X, Y, and Z respectively?
questions.explanation: Balanced equation: Al₂O₃ + 6HCl → 2AlCl₃ + 3H₂O, so X=6, Y=2, Z=3.

questions.question: Match List‑I (Natural Source) with List‑II (Acid) and select the correct answer.
questions.explanation: Vinegar → acetic acid (C). Tamarind → tartaric acid (A). Lemon → citric acid (D). Nettle sting → methanoic acid (B). So I‑C, II‑A, III‑D, IV‑B.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct arrangement of the given metals in the order of their reactivity?
questions.explanation: Reactivity series: Sodium > Magnesium > Zinc > Iron.

questions.text: What happens to the particles of a solid when its temperature is increased?

options.B: Their kinetic energy increases and they vibrate faster.
options.C: Their kinetic energy decreases and they stop moving.

questions.explanation: Increasing temperature increases kinetic energy, causing particles to vibrate faster and eventually overcome attractions.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct about evaporation?

options.A: It occurs only at the boiling point.
options.B: It can occur at any temperature below the boiling point.
options.C: It requires the liquid to be heated under high pressure.

questions.explanation: Evaporation can happen at any temperature below the boiling point, from the surface of a liquid.

questions.text: Why is cotton preferred over synthetic clothes in summer?

options.B: Cotton absorbs sweat and allows easy evaporation.

questions.explanation: Cotton absorbs sweat and facilitates evaporation, cooling the body.

questions.text: What is the key difference between a pure substance and a mixture?

options.A: Pure substances can be separated physically, mixtures cannot.
options.B: Mixtures contain more than one substance, pure substances have uniform composition.
options.C: Mixtures are always solids, pure substances are liquids.
options.D: Mixtures have chemical properties, pure substances do not.

questions.explanation: Pure substances have a fixed composition; mixtures contain two or more substances with variable composition.

questions.text: Why are alloys considered mixtures even though they cannot be separated physically?

options.B: Because they show the properties of their constituents and can have variable composition
options.D: Because they are made of pure substances only

questions.explanation: Alloys are mixtures because they have variable composition and show properties of their constituent metals.

questions.question:
Statements:
1. A solution is a homogeneous mixture.
2. The solute particles in a solution are larger than 1 micrometer.
3. A solution scatters light strongly.
4. Solute particles in a solution can be separated by filtration.
Which statements are correct?
statements: A solution is a homogeneous mixture.
The solute particles in a solution are larger than 1 micrometer.
A solution scatters light strongly.
Solute particles in a solution can be separated by filtration.
questions.explanation: Only statement 1 is correct. Solutions have particles < 1 nm, do not scatter light, and cannot be filtered.

questions.text: When sunlight enters a dark room through a small hole and the beam becomes visible, it is an example of:

options.B: Tyndall effect due to dust particles

questions.explanation: Dust particles scatter light, causing the Tyndall effect, making the beam visible.

questions.assertion: Colloids cannot be separated by filtration.
questions.reason: Colloidal particles are very small in size.

options.A: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true, but R is false.
options.D: A is false, but R is true.

questions.explanation: Colloidal particles are too small to be retained by filter paper, so they cannot be separated by filtration. R correctly explains A.

questions.text: Change of state does not involve change in:
questions.explanation: Change of state is a physical change; the chemical composition (e.g., H₂O) remains the same.

questions.text: Who first used the term 'element' in 1661?
questions.explanation: Robert Boyle was the first to use the term 'element' in 1661.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are correct?
1. Non‑metals are usually not sonorous
2. Metals have lustre
3. Non‑metals are good conductors
4. Metals can be drawn into wires
statements: Non‑metals are usually not sonorous
Metals have lustre
Non‑metals are good conductors
Metals can be drawn into wires
questions.explanation: Non‑metals are not sonorous (1 correct). Metals have lustre (2 correct). Non‑metals are poor conductors (3 incorrect). Metals are ductile (4 correct). So 1,2,4 correct.

questions.question:
Which of the following are differences between mixtures and compounds?
1. Mixtures have variable composition
2. Compounds have fixed composition
3. Mixtures form new substances
4. Compounds have different properties than elements
statements: Mixtures have variable composition
Compounds have fixed composition
Mixtures form new substances
Compounds have different properties than elements
questions.explanation: 1,2,4 are correct. Statement 3 is false – mixtures do not form new substances.

questions.text: Who stated the law of definite proportions?
questions.explanation: Joseph Proust stated the law of definite proportions (or constant composition).

questions.assertion: Atoms of different elements are identical.
questions.reason: They have different masses and chemical properties.

options.A: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.B: Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.C: A is true, but R is false.
options.D: A is false, but R is true.

questions.explanation: Atoms of different elements are different, so A is false. R is true (different masses and properties). So D.

questions.text: A group of atoms carrying a net charge is called a:
questions.explanation: A polyatomic ion is a group of atoms with a net charge (e.g., SO₄²⁻).

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about Thomson's atomic model are correct?
1. An atom consists of a positively charged sphere with electrons embedded in it.
2. The atom as a whole is electrically neutral because positive and negative charges are equal in magnitude.
statements: An atom consists of a positively charged sphere with electrons embedded in it.
The atom as a whole is electrically neutral because positive and negative charges are equal in magnitude.
questions.explanation: Thomson's model had a positive sphere with embedded electrons, and the atom is neutral. Both statements correct.

questions.text: According to Bohr's model of the atom, electrons:

options.A: Can revolve in any orbit around the nucleus
options.C: Revolve in discrete orbits without radiating energy

questions.explanation: Bohr's model: electrons revolve in fixed orbits (discrete) without radiating energy.

questions.text: Why are certain isotopes of elements used in medicine and industry?

options.A: Because their chemical properties are different from the element
options.B: Because some isotopes have special physical or nuclear properties
options.C: Because isotopes are cheaper to produce
options.D: Because isotopes change the chemical reactions of elements

questions.explanation: Isotopes like Co‑60, I‑131 have special nuclear properties used in medicine and industry.

questions.text: Native minerals are:

options.A: Compounds made of two or more elements
options.B: Pure elements, either metals or non‑metals

questions.explanation: Native minerals are pure elements in nature (e.g., gold, silver, sulfur).

questions.text: Who proposed the Law of Octaves in 1865?
questions.explanation: Newlands proposed the Law of Octaves in 1865.

questions.text: Proteins that act as biological catalysts in the body are called:
questions.explanation: Enzymes are proteins that catalyse biochemical reactions.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. Chlordiazepoxide and meprobamate are mild tranquilizers used to relieve tension.
2. Equanil is used to treat infections.
statements: Chlordiazepoxide and meprobamate are mild tranquilizers used to relieve tension.
Equanil is used to treat infections.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct (tranquilizers). Statement 2 is false – Equanil is used for depression/hypertension, not infections.

questions.text: Which of the following is a bacteriostatic antibiotic?
questions.explanation: Tetracycline inhibits bacterial growth (bacteriostatic). Penicillin, aminoglycosides, ofloxacin are bactericidal.

questions.text: Which substance is added to shaving soaps to prevent rapid drying?
questions.explanation: Glycerol is a humectant added to shaving soaps to retain moisture and prevent drying.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
1. Novolac is a cross‑linked polymer.
2. Novolac on heating with formaldehyde forms bakelite.
statements: Novolac is a cross‑linked polymer.
Novolac on heating with formaldehyde forms bakelite.
questions.explanation: Novolac is a linear polymer (not cross‑linked). It becomes cross‑linked with formaldehyde to form bakelite. So only statement 2 is correct.

questions.text: Acid used for hydrolysis of sucrose to glucose can be:
questions.explanation: Dilute HCl or H₂SO₄ is used to hydrolyse sucrose to glucose and fructose.

questions.text: Nucleic acids are polymers of:
questions.explanation: Nucleic acids (DNA/RNA) are polymers of nucleotides.

questions.text: The boiling points of aldehydes and ketones are generally:

options.A: Higher than alcohols of similar molecular mass
options.B: Lower than hydrocarbons of similar molecular mass
options.C: Higher than hydrocarbons and ethers but lower than alcohols of similar molecular mass

questions.explanation: Aldehydes and ketones have dipole‑dipole interactions, so their boiling points are higher than hydrocarbons and ethers, but lower than alcohols (which have hydrogen bonding).

questions.text: In alcohols, the boiling point:

options.A: Increases with branching of the carbon chain
options.B: Decreases with branching of the carbon chain
options.C: Is independent of carbon chain length
options.D: Decreases with increase in number of carbon atoms

questions.explanation: Branching reduces surface area and van der Waals forces, decreasing boiling point.

questions.text: Henry's law states that at a constant temperature, the solubility of a gas in a liquid is:

options.B: Inversely proportional to the partial pressure of the gas
options.C: Directly proportional to the partial pressure of the gas
options.D: Dependent only on the nature of the solvent

questions.explanation: Henry's law: solubility of a gas is directly proportional to its partial pressure (p = K_H x).

questions.text: Goldsmiths blow the outermost zone of a flame with a metallic blowpipe for melting gold and silver. For which one of the following reasons do they use the outermost zone of the flame?

options.A: It is the hottest part providing the most heat.
options.B: This luminous zone doesn't produce any ash.
options.C: It is the least hottest part protecting the design of jewellery.
options.D: Middle zone vaporises gold, leading to loss of metal.

questions.explanation: The outermost zone is the hottest (complete combustion) and is used for melting metals.

questions.text:
Consider the following scenario:
Two inflated balloons are rubbed with the same woollen cloth and brought close to each other. It is observed that the balloons repel each other. In the above scenario, which force is exerted by one of the charged balloons on the other charged balloon?
questions.explanation: The balloons acquire same charge (both negative), so they repel each other via electrostatic force.

questions.text: Lactometers, which are used for determining the purity of milk, work based on which of the following principles/laws?
questions.explanation: Lactometers measure density based on buoyancy (Archimedes' principle).

questions.text: Water can dissolve more substances than any other liquid because:

options.B: It is a good conductor of heat
options.C: It has a high value of specific heat
options.D: It is an oxide of hydrogen

questions.explanation: Water's polarity makes it a good solvent for ionic and polar compounds.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
Statement I: A bar magnet, when suspended freely, has its north and south poles of the magnet point approximately towards the geographic north and south poles, respectively.
Statement II: The force of attraction and repulsion of the magnet is highest at its poles.
Which one of the following is correct with respect to the above statements?
statements: A bar magnet, when suspended freely, has its north and south poles of the magnet point approximately towards the geographic north and south poles, respectively.
The force of attraction and repulsion of the magnet is highest at its poles.

options.A: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, and Statement II explains Statement I
options.B: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, and Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.C: Statement I is correct, but Statement II is not correct
options.D: Statement I is not correct, but Statement II is correct

questions.explanation: Both statements are correct, but II (poles have strongest force) does not explain why a magnet aligns north‑south (that is due to Earth's magnetic field).

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
Statement I: The walls of a large dam are designed with a much wider base compared to their top.
Statement II: The horizontal pressure exerted by water on a dam wall increases with an increase in the depth of water.
Which of the above is correct with respect to the above statements?
statements: The walls of a large dam are designed with a much wider base compared to their top.
The horizontal pressure exerted by water on a dam wall increases with an increase in the depth of water.

options.A: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, and Statement II explains Statement I.
options.B: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, but Statement II does not explain Statement I.
options.C: Statement I is correct, but Statement II is not correct.
options.D: Statement I is not correct, but Statement II is correct.

questions.explanation: Pressure increases with depth (P = ρgh), so the base must be thicker to withstand higher pressure. II correctly explains I.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements with reference to yeasts:
I. They belong to a group of microorganisms called fungi.
II. Carbon dioxide is evolved from food materials spoiled by yeasts.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
statements: They belong to a group of microorganisms called fungi.
Carbon dioxide is evolved from food materials spoiled by yeasts.
questions.explanation: Yeasts are fungi (I correct). They respire anaerobically, producing CO₂ (II correct).

questions.question:
With reference to diseases and their causative agents, consider the following pairs:
Disease
I. Measles
II. Cholera
III. Typhoid
IV. Malaria
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
statements: Measles – Virus
Cholera – Worms
Typhoid – Bacteria
Malaria – Protozoa
questions.explanation: Measles (virus) correct. Cholera (bacteria, not worms) incorrect. Typhoid (bacteria) correct. Malaria (protozoa) correct. So 3 correct.

questions.text: When iodine solution is added to a food substance and it turns blue‑black in colour, this reaction primarily indicates the presence of which of the following in a food substance?
questions.explanation: Iodine test is specific for starch (blue‑black colour).

questions.question:
Consider the following cell organelles:
I. Cell wall
II. Plastids
III. Golgi apparatus
IV. Cytoplasm
How many of the above are generally present in plant cells but absent in animal cells?
statements: Cell wall
Plastids
Golgi apparatus
Cytoplasm
questions.explanation: Cell wall and plastids are unique to plant cells (2). Golgi and cytoplasm are present in both.

questions.question:
With reference to differences between xylem and phloem tissues in plants, which of the following statements is/are correct?
I. Xylem primarily transports water and minerals from roots to other parts of the plant, whereas phloem primarily transports prepared food from leaves to different parts of the plant.
II. Transport through xylem is mainly bidirectional, whereas transport through phloem is always unidirectional.
III. Most of the structural elements of xylem are dead at maturity, while most structural elements of phloem are living tissues.
statements: Xylem primarily transports water and minerals from roots to other parts of the plant, whereas phloem primarily transports prepared food from leaves to different parts of the plant.
Transport through xylem is mainly bidirectional, whereas transport through phloem is always unidirectional.
Most of the structural elements of xylem are dead at maturity, while most structural elements of phloem are living tissues.
questions.explanation: I and III are correct. II is incorrect: xylem transport is unidirectional (upward), phloem is bidirectional.

questions.question:
With reference to the ongoing war in West Asia, which of the following statements with respect to the differences between ballistic missiles and cruise missiles is/are incorrect?
I. Compared to Ballistic missiles, Cruise missiles are generally harder to detect by radar.
II. Cruise missiles can only be used to deliver conventional warheads, whereas ballistic missiles can carry both nuclear and conventional warheads.
statements: Compared to Ballistic missiles, Cruise missiles are generally harder to detect by radar.
Cruise missiles can only be used to deliver conventional warheads, whereas ballistic missiles can carry both nuclear and conventional warheads.
questions.explanation: I is correct (cruise missiles are harder to detect). II is incorrect – both types can carry nuclear and conventional warheads.

questions.text: What is the primary function of a moderator in a nuclear reactor?

options.A: To absorb excess neutrons and stop the chain reaction.
options.B: To convert nuclear energy directly into electrical energy.
options.C: To slow down fast‑moving neutrons produced during nuclear fission.
options.D: To remove heat from the reactor core to prevent it from excessive heating.

questions.explanation: A moderator slows down fast neutrons to sustain a chain reaction. Common moderators: graphite, heavy water.

questions.question:
Recently, the Kalpakkam fast breeder reactor attained criticality. With reference to the above news, consider the following statements:
Statement I: Naturally occurring thorium can be directly used as fuel in nuclear reactors for the generation of electrical power.
Statement II: Naturally occurring thorium is a 'fertile' material that can be converted into a 'fissile material.'
Which one of the following is correct with respect to the above statements?
statements: Naturally occurring thorium can be directly used as fuel in nuclear reactors for the generation of electrical power.
Naturally occurring thorium is a 'fertile' material that can be converted into a 'fissile material.'

options.A: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, and Statement II explains Statement I
options.B: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.C: Statement I is correct, but Statement II is not correct
options.D: Statement I is not correct, but Statement II is correct

questions.explanation: Thorium cannot be used directly (I incorrect). It is fertile and can be converted to U‑233 (II correct). So D.

questions.question:
Consider the following diseases:
I. Sickle‑cell anaemia
II. Thalassemia
III. Tuberculosis
IV. Phenylketonuria
How many of the above are generally referred to as genetic disorders?
statements: Sickle‑cell anaemia
Thalassemia
Tuberculosis
Phenylketonuria
questions.explanation: Sickle‑cell anaemia, Thalassemia, and Phenylketonuria are genetic disorders. Tuberculosis is infectious. So 3.

questions.text: In amoeba, food is digested in which part?
questions.explanation: Food is digested inside the food vacuole by digestive enzymes.

questions.text: In which mode of nutrition an organism derives food from the body of another living organism without killing it?
questions.explanation: Parasitic nutrition involves deriving nutrients from a living host without immediately killing it.

questions.text: Which part of nephron allows the selective re‑absorption of useful substances into the blood capillaries?
questions.explanation: Selective re‑absorption occurs in the renal tubule (proximal convoluted tubule, loop of Henle, etc.).

questions.question:
Consider the following statements.
I. Pyruvate may be converted into ethanol and carbon dioxide in presence of oxygen.
II. Pyruvate may be converted into carbon dioxide and water in absence of oxygen.
III. Pyruvate may be converted into lactic acid in lack of oxygen.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?
statements: Pyruvate may be converted into ethanol and carbon dioxide in presence of oxygen.
Pyruvate may be converted into carbon dioxide and water in absence of oxygen.
Pyruvate may be converted into lactic acid in lack of oxygen.
questions.explanation: I is false (ethanol + CO₂ occurs in absence of oxygen – anaerobic respiration). II is false (CO₂ + water occurs in presence of oxygen – aerobic). III is correct (lactic acid in lack of oxygen). So only III.

questions.question:
Consider the following.
I. Dendrite – Information is acquired
II. Cell body – Impulse converted into a chemical signal
III. Axon end – Information travels as an electrical impulse
Which of the above is/are correctly matched?
statements: Dendrite – Information is acquired
Cell body – Impulse converted into a chemical signal
Axon end – Information travels as an electrical impulse
questions.explanation: I is correct (dendrites receive signals). II is incorrect (conversion to chemical signal occurs at synapse, not cell body). III is incorrect (axon end releases chemical signals; electrical impulse travels along axon).

questions.question:
Consider the following statements.
I. The DNA in the cell nucleus is the information source for making proteins.
II. Different proteins eventually lead to altered body designs.
Identify the correct statement/s.
statements: The DNA in the cell nucleus is the information source for making proteins.
Different proteins eventually lead to altered body designs.
questions.explanation: DNA directs protein synthesis (I correct). Variation in proteins can lead to changes in traits (II correct).

questions.text: The consistency of DNA copying during reproduction is important

options.C: for the maintenance of body design features.

questions.explanation: Consistency ensures inheritance of the same body design and traits, maintaining species characteristics.

questions.text: Which of the following is true for Vegetatively propagated plants?
questions.explanation: Vegetative propagation produces clones, genetically identical to the parent.

questions.text: The main function of abscisic acid in plants is

options.D: To increase the length of cells.

questions.explanation: Abscisic acid is a growth inhibitor, promotes dormancy and stress responses.

questions.text: Salivation is controlled by which part of the brain?
questions.explanation: Salivation is an involuntary reflex controlled by the medulla oblongata.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the basic parts of a cell:
1. The cell membrane controls the entry and exit of substances into the cell.
2. The nucleus regulates cell activities and carries hereditary information.
3. The cytoplasm is the site of most biochemical reactions.
4. The cell wall is present in both plant and animal cells, providing rigidity.
Which of the above are correct?
statements: The cell membrane controls the entry and exit of substances into the cell.
The nucleus regulates cell activities and carries hereditary information.
The cytoplasm is the site of most biochemical reactions.
The cell wall is present in both plant and animal cells, providing rigidity.
questions.explanation: 1,2,3 are correct. 4 is false – cell wall is absent in animal cells.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about plant and animal cells:
1. Plastids are tiny rod‑shaped structures found in plant cells, some of which (chloroplasts) contain chlorophyll for photosynthesis.
2. In non‑green parts of plants, plastids help in storage of substances.
3. Plant cells have a large vacuole that stores substances, removes waste, and maintains cell shape.
4. Animal cells always have large vacuoles similar to plant cells.
Which of the above are correct?
statements: Plastids are tiny rod‑shaped structures found in plant cells, some of which (chloroplasts) contain chlorophyll for photosynthesis.
In non‑green parts of plants, plastids help in storage of substances.
Plant cells have a large vacuole that stores substances, removes waste, and maintains cell shape.
Animal cells always have large vacuoles similar to plant cells.
questions.explanation: 1,2,3 correct. 4 is false – animal cells have small or no vacuoles.

questions.text: Which of the following features helps neurons (nerve cells) to transmit messages quickly in the body?
questions.explanation: Neurons have long axons and branched dendrites to transmit impulses quickly over distances.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct sequence of levels of organisation in a living organism?

options.A: Organ → Tissue → Cell → Organ system → Organism
options.B: Cell → Tissue → Organ → Organ system → Organism
options.C: Tissue → Cell → Organ → Organism → Organ system
options.D: Cell → Organ → Tissue → Organ system → Organism

questions.explanation: The correct hierarchy is Cell → Tissue → Organ → Organ System → Organism.

questions.question:
Match the Following
Column A (Microorganism)
1. Amoeba (Protozoa)
2. Mould (Fungi)
3. Algae
4. Bacteria
Column B (Characteristic)
A. Branched filament without chlorophyll, brush‑like structure
B. Single cell, irregular shape, moves
C. Spherical, presence of chlorophyll (green pigment)
D. Spherical/comma/spiral/rod shaped, with hair‑like projections

columnB.value: Branched filament without chlorophyll, brush‑like structure

questions.explanation: Amoeba – irregular single cell (B). Mould – branched filaments (A). Algae – chlorophyll (C). Bacteria – various shapes with projections (D). So 1‑B, 2‑A, 3‑C, 4‑D.

questions.text: Why are leguminous crops like peas and beans often grown in crop rotation?

options.A: They increase water content in the soil
options.B: They help in nitrogen fixation through bacteria in root nodules
options.D: They reduce sunlight requirement for crops

questions.explanation: Legumes have Rhizobium bacteria in root nodules that fix atmospheric nitrogen, enriching soil fertility.

questions.text: Spirulina is called a superfood mainly because it is rich in:
questions.explanation: Spirulina contains 60‑70% protein, making it a rich protein source.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about diseases in India:
1. Non‑communicable diseases (NCDs) such as cancer, diabetes, and asthma are not caused by pathogens and do not spread from person to person.
2. Communicable diseases are caused by pathogens and can spread from one person to another.
3. The rise of NCDs in India is linked to lifestyle changes such as processed food consumption, reduced physical activity, and longer lifespans.
4. Today, most deaths in India are caused by communicable diseases.
Which of the above statements are correct?
statements: Non‑communicable diseases (NCDs) such as cancer, diabetes, and asthma are not caused by pathogens and do not spread from person to person.
Communicable diseases are caused by pathogens and can spread from one person to another.
The rise of NCDs in India is linked to lifestyle changes such as processed food consumption, reduced physical activity, and longer lifespans.
Today, most deaths in India are caused by communicable diseases.
questions.explanation: 1,2,3 are correct. 4 is false – NCDs now cause the majority of deaths in India.

questions.question:
Match the Following:
List – I (Disease)
A. Measles
B. Tuberculosis (TB)
C. Ascariasis
D. Malaria
Column B (Causative Agent)
1. Protozoa
2. Bacteria
3. Worm
4. Virus
questions.explanation: Measles – Virus (4), TB – Bacteria (2), Ascariasis – Worm (3), Malaria – Protozoa (1).

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about antibiotics and antibiotics‑resistance:
1. Antibiotics are effective against bacterial infections and have saved millions of lives since their discovery.
2. Indiscriminate use of antibiotics has led to the phenomenon of antibiotic resistance.
3. Antibiotic resistance makes common infections easier to treat and reduces complications
4. Antibiotic resistance occurs when bacteria survive and multiply despite treatment with a given antibiotic.
Which of the above statements are correct?
statements: Antibiotics are effective against bacterial infections and have saved millions of lives since their discovery.
Indiscriminate use of antibiotics has led to the phenomenon of antibiotic resistance.
Antibiotic resistance makes common infections easier to treat and reduces complications.
Antibiotic resistance occurs when bacteria survive and multiply despite treatment with a given antibiotic.
questions.explanation: 1,2,4 correct. 3 is false – resistance makes infections harder to treat.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding pollination in flowering plants is/are correct?
1. Pollination is the transfer of pollen grains from stamens to carpels.
2. Pollination always results in fertilisation.
3. Agents like wind, insects, birds, and bats can help in pollination.
statements: Pollination is the transfer of pollen grains from stamens to carpels.
Pollination always results in fertilisation.
Agents like wind, insects, birds, and bats can help in pollination.
questions.explanation: 1 and 3 are correct. 2 is false – pollination does not guarantee fertilisation.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about ecosystems is/are correct?
1. An ecosystem consists of interactions between biotic and abiotic components.
2. Aquatic ecosystems include forests and grasslands.
3. Farmland is an example of a human‑made ecosystem.
statements: An ecosystem consists of interactions between biotic and abiotic components.
Aquatic ecosystems include forests and grasslands.
Farmland is an example of a human‑made ecosystem.
questions.explanation: 1 correct. 2 incorrect – forests/grasslands are terrestrial. 3 correct – farmland is human‑made. So 1 and 3.

questions.question:
Match the following types of interactions with their correct definitions:
List – I (Type of Interaction)
A. Mutualism
B. Commensalism
C. Parasitism
Column B (Definition)
1. One organism exploits the host for resources, causing harm without immediate death
2. Reciprocal interdependence ensuring survival and fitness of both organisms
3. One organism derives benefit without measurable gain or loss to the other

columnB.value: One organism exploits the host for resources, causing harm without immediate death
Reciprocal interdependence ensuring survival and fitness of both organisms
One organism derives benefit without measurable gain or loss to the other

questions.explanation: Mutualism (2), Commensalism (3), Parasitism (1).

questions.text: What is meant by geodiversity?

options.A: Variety of living organisms on Earth
options.B: Variety of landforms, rocks, soils and the processes shaping them
options.C: Study of climate and weather patterns

questions.explanation: Geodiversity refers to the variety of non‑living physical features (landforms, rocks, soils) and the processes that shape them.

questions.text: In animals, the development of the zygote into an embryo can occur either inside or outside the body. Which of the following statements best explains the evolutionary advantage of internal development compared to external development?

options.A: Internal development ensures genetic variation in offspring.
options.B: Internal development provides better protection and nourishment to the developing embryo.
options.C: Internal development allows offspring to be exact copies of the parent.
options.D: Internal development reduces the need for sexual reproduction.

questions.explanation: Internal development offers better protection and nourishment, increasing survival chances of the embryo.

questions.question:
Match the Following: List‑I with List‑II.
List – I (Scientist)
A. Robert Hooke
B. Antonie van Leeuwenhoek
C. Robert Brown
D. Purkinje
E. Schleiden & Schwann
List – II (Contribution)
1. Coined the term 'protoplasm' for the living substance of the cell
2. First observed cells in a cork slice using a primitive microscope (1665)
3. Discovered free‑living microscopic organisms in pond water (1674)
4. Discovered the nucleus in the cell (1831)
5. Proposed that all plants and animals are composed of cells (Cell Theory)

columnB.value: Coined the term 'protoplasm' for the living substance of the cell
First observed cells in a cork slice using a primitive microscope (1665)
Discovered free‑living microscopic organisms in pond water (1674)
Discovered the nucleus in the cell (1831)
Proposed that all plants and animals are composed of cells (Cell Theory)

questions.explanation: Hooke – 2, Leeuwenhoek – 3, Brown – 4, Purkinje – 1, Schleiden & Schwann – 5.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about diffusion and osmosis is correct?

options.A: Diffusion always requires energy input from the cell.
options.B: Oxygen enters the cell by diffusion when its concentration inside the cell decreases.
options.C: Osmosis refers to the movement of gases like CO₂ and O₂ across the cell membrane.
options.D: Diffusion occurs only in water molecules, while osmosis occurs in all substances.

questions.explanation: Diffusion is passive, no energy required. Oxygen diffuses into the cell when its concentration inside is lower. Osmosis is specifically water movement across a membrane. So B is correct.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding osmosis is/are correct?
1. In a hypotonic solution, water enters the cell and it may swell.
2. In a hypertonic solution, water leaves the cell causing it to shrink.
3. In an isotonic solution, water moves across the membrane but there is no net movement.
statements: In a hypotonic solution, water enters the cell and it may swell.
In a hypertonic solution, water leaves the cell causing it to shrink.
In an isotonic solution, water moves across the membrane but there is no net movement.
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct descriptions of osmosis in hypotonic, hypertonic, and isotonic solutions.

questions.text: What is the process called by which a cell engulfs food or other materials from its surroundings?
questions.explanation: Endocytosis is the process of engulfing materials by invagination of the cell membrane.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about chromosomes and DNA is correct?

options.A: Chromosomes are visible at all times in the cell.
options.B: Chromosomes are composed only of DNA molecules.
options.C: Genes are functional segments of DNA that carry hereditary information.
options.D: Chromatin material is visible only during cell division.

questions.explanation: Genes are segments of DNA that carry hereditary information. Chromosomes are visible only during division; they contain DNA and proteins. Chromatin is visible in non‑dividing cells.

questions.text: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes prokaryotic cells from eukaryotic cells?

options.A: Prokaryotic cells have a well‑defined nuclear membrane, while eukaryotic cells lack one.
options.B: Prokaryotic cells lack a nuclear membrane and most cytoplasmic organelles, while eukaryotic cells possess them.
options.C: Prokaryotic cells contain plastids for photosynthesis, while eukaryotic cells use vesicles.
options.D: Prokaryotic cells cannot perform any cellular functions without organelles.

questions.explanation: Prokaryotes lack a nucleus and membrane‑bound organelles; eukaryotes have both.

questions.text: Which of the following statements correctly explains why viruses are not considered fully living organisms?

options.A: Viruses can photosynthesize and produce their own energy.
options.B: Viruses have cell membranes and organelles like living cells.
options.C: Viruses lack membranes and cannot carry out life processes independently.
options.D: Viruses can move and respire outside a host.

questions.explanation: Viruses lack cellular structures and cannot carry out metabolism or reproduction independently; they require a host cell.

questions.text: Which statement best describes the general role of the endoplasmic reticulum (ER)?

options.A: ER is a network of membranes involved in protein and lipid synthesis, as well as in membrane formation and enzyme/hormone production.
options.B: ER only stores waste and toxins within the cell.
options.D: ER is a rigid structure and does not form a network in cells.

questions.explanation: ER synthesises proteins (RER) and lipids (SER), and is involved in membrane biogenesis and enzyme/hormone production.

questions.text: How does meiosis differ from mitosis?

options.A: Meiosis produces two identical cells, mitosis produces four different cells
options.B: Meiosis occurs in body cells, mitosis occurs in reproductive cells
options.C: Meiosis produces four cells with half the chromosomes, mitosis produces two identical cells
options.D: Meiosis does not involve cell division, mitosis does

questions.explanation: Meiosis produces four haploid cells; mitosis produces two diploid identical cells.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about stomata is correct?

options.A: Stomata are pores in the leaf epidermis, controlled by guard cells, and are essential for gaseous exchange.
options.B: Stomata are present only in roots and help in absorption of water.
options.C: Stomata are pores that allow only water vapour to escape but not gases.
options.D: Stomata are pores that remain permanently open for continuous exchange of gases.

questions.explanation: Stomata are epidermal pores regulated by guard cells, allowing gas exchange and transpiration.

questions.text: In some plants, like desert plants, the epidermis has a thick waxy coating of cutin on its outer surface. This adaptation helps the plant to:

options.A: Absorb more water from the soil
options.B: Reduce water loss due to evaporation

questions.explanation: The waxy cuticle reduces water loss by evaporation, an adaptation for arid environments.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is true for both xylem and phloem?

options.A: Both are complex tissues forming vascular bundles
options.C: Both consist entirely of dead cells
options.D: Both are simple tissues made of one cell type

questions.explanation: Both xylem and phloem are complex tissues that together form vascular bundles.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes blood?

options.A: Blood is a solid tissue without any fluid component.
options.B: Blood has a fluid matrix called plasma, in which RBCs, WBCs, and platelets are suspended.
options.C: Blood only transports oxygen and nothing else.
options.D: Blood consists entirely of living cells without any proteins or salts.

questions.explanation: Blood is a connective tissue with plasma (liquid matrix) containing blood cells and platelets.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about bone is correct?

options.A: Bone is a flexible connective tissue mainly used for storing fat.
options.B: Bone is a connective tissue with cells embedded in a hard matrix of calcium and phosphorus, providing support and anchoring muscles.
options.C: Bone has a liquid matrix like blood.
options.D: Bone cells are not embedded in any matrix and are free‑floating.

questions.explanation: Bone has a hard matrix of calcium and phosphorus, provides support and muscle attachment.

questions.text: Which connective tissue connects muscles to bones and has great strength but limited flexibility?
questions.explanation: Tendons connect muscle to bone; they are strong and inelastic.
